name: Fintech Onboarding
definition_origin: Category pattern crystallized 2014-2024 by the wave of consumer fintech apps that scaled past $1B valuation: Acorns (2012, $2B), Robinhood (2013, IPO 2021), Cash App (2013, Block subsidiary $40B+ revenue 2024), Chime (2013, $25B valuation), Venmo (2009, PayPal acquisition $50M revenue 2014→$1.4B 2024), SoFi (2011, IPO 2021), N26 / Revolut / Monzo (Europe 2013-2015 wave). Codified by KYC/AML compliance frameworks (Bank Secrecy Act, FinCEN, OFAC, EU AMLD5/AMLD6, UK FCA). Onboarding doctrine sources: Onfido / Persona / Plaid integration playbooks; Sumsub / Veriff KYC orchestration patterns; Acorns onboarding teardowns (designerup.co, Mobbin); Robinhood's 'instant deposit' activation pattern; Cash App's '$1 send' first-action doctrine. The dominant doctrine: trust-building screens first, then KYC, then aha-moment funded-account, with biometric gate (item 61) replacing passwords for repeat sessions. Onboarding success defined narrowly as 'funded account' (Acorns) or 'first-transfer' (Cash App, Venmo), NOT engagement metrics.
core_mechanism: Fintech onboarding works by inverting the consumer 'minimize friction' default — financial trust is BUILT through visible, deliberate friction (KYC, identity verification, bank-grade security copy) which is psychologically REASSURING in this category. Five reinforcing forces: (1) Trust-as-prerequisite — users will not enter SSN/bank credentials unless the app feels institutionally serious; minimal-onboarding-doctrine BACKFIRES here. (2) Compliance-as-feature — KYC steps (ID upload, selfie verification, address proof) double as trust signals when framed transparently ('Required by federal regulations to keep your money safe'). (3) Activation defined narrowly — 'funded account' (Acorns), 'first-transfer' (Cash App), 'first-trade' (Robinhood) — not engagement metrics. (4) Biometric replaces password — Face ID / Touch ID / passkeys (item 61) is the 2025-2026 default; passwords feel insecure in financial context. (5) Educational interleaving — fintech onboarding INSTRUCTS during friction (Acorns 'How investing works' mid-KYC) to convert wait-time into perceived-value-time. Sage archetype dominates (financial wisdom = trust); Ruler secondary (institutional authority); Caregiver tertiary (financial care).
neuroscience_correlate: Trust calibration via visible institutional cues activates ventromedial PFC (Delgado 2005). Loss-aversion prominence in fintech (Kahneman & Tversky 1979) — users hyper-attend to security copy. Authority cues (lock icons, bank-grade language) trigger heuristic-trust shortcuts (Cialdini Authority principle). Biometric authentication leverages familiar somatosensory loops (Face ID = facial-recognition cortex / fusiform face area), reducing cognitive load vs password recall (working memory, dorsolateral PFC).
empirical_evidence: Acorns onboarding (designerup.co teardown 2023): success defined as 'funded account', achieved by 70%+ of installs via tight 8-screen flow with educational interleaving; $5B+ AUM by 2024. Robinhood instant-deposit activation: users who deposited within first session had 80% D30 retention vs 25% non-funded (Robinhood S-1 filing 2021). Cash App $1-send: first-transfer < 5 minutes is the activation threshold; D30 retention >75% for completers (Block earnings 2023-2024). Plaid 2024 study (Plaid State of Fintech Onboarding): bank-link via Plaid Link integration achieves 75% completion vs 45% for manual routing/account input. Onfido / Sumsub 2024 KYC benchmarks: combined ID + selfie verification completion rates 85-92% with optimized UI; drops to 60% with poor UX. Persona 2024: identity-verification abandonment correlates with screen-count (>5 screens = 30% drop). Chime 2023: biometric-gate adoption 88% within first week (Face ID / Touch ID). EU Open Banking PSD2 (2018+) onboarding via TPP/AISP integrations sees 65-75% completion. PCI DSS compliance + SOC 2 Type II are baseline expectations. Klarna Pay-in-4 onboarding: 1-screen approval with deferred KYC for low-amount transactions.
measurable_outcome_metric: Funded-account rate (% installs that fund within first session — Acorns standard, target ≥40%). Time-to-first-transaction (target <5 min — Cash App standard). KYC completion rate (target ≥85% via Onfido/Persona/Sumsub). Bank-link conversion (target ≥75% via Plaid). Biometric-gate adoption (target ≥85% within D7). D30 retention for funded-vs-non-funded (cleavage metric). Customer LTV (fintech LTVs are highest in subscription categories — $200-2000+ depending on product). KYC fraud-rejection rate (must balance against false-positive friction).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Robinhood funded-vs-non-funded D30: 80% vs 25% (3.2× retention lift). Plaid Link vs manual bank entry: 75% vs 45% completion (1.7× lift). Onfido optimized vs default KYC UI: 92% vs 60% completion (1.5× lift). Cash App $1-send activation: D30 >75% for completers vs 35% non-completers. Biometric-gate adoption (Chime): 88% week-one. Educational interleaving during KYC (Acorns): reduces KYC abandonment by ~25 percentage points (case study).
engineering_tactics: DEFAULT PATTERN: Trust-First Sequenced Onboarding — minimal onboarding pre-KYC, then mandatory KYC with educational interleaving, then aha funded-account. SPECIFIC SEQUENCE: (1) WELCOME + value proposition (1-2 screens) — Sage register, NOT story-arc. (2) AUTH — magic link (item 60) or Sign in with Apple / Google. NEVER password as default in 2025-2026 fintech. (3) BIOMETRIC GATE setup (item 61) — Face ID / Touch ID / passkey enrollment immediately post-auth. (4) PHONE VERIFICATION — SMS OTP for fraud reduction. (5) BASIC KYC — name, DOB, address, last-4 SSN (US). 6. INTERLEAVED EDUCATION — 'While we verify, here's how investing works' (Acorns pattern) — converts wait-time into value. 7. DOCUMENT KYC — ID upload (drivers-license / passport) + selfie via Onfido/Persona/Sumsub iframe or SDK. 8. BANK LINK — Plaid Link (US), Tink/TrueLayer (EU/UK), Yodlee (international fallback). 9. FUND ACCOUNT — minimum-deposit prompt as the engineered aha. 10. POST-FUNDING dashboard with first-feature CTA. FSM: XState v5 with deep persistence (KYC abandoned mid-flow must resume — backend source of truth). Encryption: TLS 1.3 + at-rest encryption mandatory. Audit log: every KYC step logged for FinCEN / SAR compliance. Anti-fraud: device fingerprinting (Sift, Persona), IP geolocation, velocity checks. Forbid: 33-step consumer questionnaire (out of doctrine — wastes the user's compliance patience), AI-plan-reveal at climax (gimmicky in financial context), aggressive paywall-at-onboarding (financial subscriptions deferred to feature-gating).
real_world_examples: (1) Acorns: 8-screen onboarding — welcome → auth → SSN/DOB → bank link via Plaid → 'Round Ups' explainer (educational interleaving) → first-investment commit ($5 minimum) → funded account. Total 6-8 minutes. (2) Robinhood: auth → identity verification (SSN, DOB, address) → suitability questionnaire (FINRA-required) → bank link → instant-deposit prompt (the engineered aha — first-trade as activation). (3) Cash App: phone verification → name → optional bank link → $1 send-money first-action prompt within first session. Aggressive minimal — KYC deferred until larger amounts. (4) Chime: phone → Face ID enrollment → SSN → identity verification (Persona/Onfido) → debit card auto-issued → bank link OR direct-deposit setup. Funded account benchmark. (5) Venmo: phone → Face ID → bank link → first-payment request as activation. (6) N26 / Revolut / Monzo (EU): video selfie KYC (PSD2 strong customer authentication) → IBAN provisioning → first-transfer as aha. Stricter KYC than US doctrine. (7) Plaid Link integration: 4-screen embedded flow (search bank → credentials → MFA → confirm). (8) Onfido / Persona SDK: 3-screen ID verification (capture front → capture back → selfie liveness). (9) Klarna Pay-in-4: deferred-KYC for low-amount, full KYC trigger at threshold. (10) SoFi: education-heavy onboarding, FINRA-suitability questionnaire framed as financial-planning.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary in 2025-2026 consumer fintech (Acorns, Cash App, Robinhood, Chime, Venmo all mobile-first; web is companion). Mobile leverages camera for ID/selfie KYC (mandatory for liveness checks), Face ID/Touch ID for biometric gate, push notifications for transaction confirmations, deep-link from bank-OAuth callback. Web equivalent: webcam-based KYC less reliable; biometric gate via WebAuthn passkeys; bank-link via Plaid Link iframe identical UX. EU PSD2 strong customer authentication requires BOTH possession (device) + inherence (biometric) factors — mobile satisfies this natively. Web fintech onboarding (Wealthfront, Betterment) sees ~25% lower completion than mobile equivalents.
platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) translucent material risks UX failure in fintech — financial users associate transparency/blur with insecurity. Solid backgrounds + bank-grade typography preferred. Liquid Glass alert sheets are acceptable for IAP-style disclosures (subscription tiers in SoFi+ Plus). M3 Expressive (Android 16+) shape system allows distinctive fintech branding (Cash App's monochrome ink-on-mint, Chime's lime accent). Both DLs handle biometric gate (Face ID / Fingerprint) natively. Fintech category specifically benefits from RESTRAINED design language — Sage/Ruler register, monochrome with single accent (Acorns green, Robinhood ember, Chime mint, N26 black-and-white), no gradient (gradient on financial UI signals 'consumer-app gimmick' to risk-averse users).
category_fit: Strongest fit: neobanks (Chime, N26, Revolut, Monzo, SoFi), payments (Cash App, Venmo, Zelle, Wise), investing (Robinhood, Acorns, Public, Wealthfront, Betterment, Stash), BNPL (Klarna, Affirm, Afterpay), crypto (Coinbase, Kraken, Phantom — though crypto compliance differs), insurance (Lemonade, Hippo, Root), lending (Affirm, Upstart). EXCLUSIONS: B2B fintech (Mercury, Ramp, Brex) uses productivity-onboarding doctrine (item 55) NOT consumer fintech, despite financial domain — buyers are workspaces. Pure-utility budgeting apps (YNAB, Copilot Money, Monarch Money) without bank-account-funding action use a hybrid wellness/productivity pattern.
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_month minimum — KYC integration alone is multi-week (Onfido/Persona/Sumsub SDK + audit-log compliance + state-resume). 3-month realistic for full Acorns-equivalent onboarding. Solo-dev fintech is rare due to: licensure requirements (BSA money-transmitter licenses, FINRA broker-dealer if investing), banking-as-a-service partner cost (Synapse before bankruptcy 2024, Unit, Treasury Prime, Lithic, Highnote), KYC vendor cost ($1-3 per check). Solo-dev viable for: budgeting (YNAB-like) without funded-account; crypto wallets (less licensure but post-FTX KYC tightened); BNPL via Klarna/Affirm SDK partnership.
tooling_options: KYC/Identity: Onfido (most expensive, gold standard), Persona (mid-market, dev-friendly API), Sumsub (international/EM), Veriff, Stripe Identity, Plaid Identity. Bank linking: Plaid (US gold standard), Tink (EU), TrueLayer (UK/EU), Yodlee (legacy international), MX. Banking-as-a-Service: Unit, Treasury Prime, Lithic, Highnote, Stripe Treasury. Auth: Auth0 + Auth0 Enterprise, Clerk (limited fintech use), Stytch, Magic.link, Supabase Auth + custom KYC. Anti-fraud: Sift, Persona Anti-Fraud, Alloy (KYC orchestration). Compliance/audit: Onfido + Persona include audit logs; Comply Advantage (sanctions screening); ChainAlysis (crypto). Push: OneSignal, Plotline (rare in fintech). Card-issuing: Marqeta, Stripe Issuing, Lithic, Highnote.
ai_dependency: none for the core flow — fintech onboarding doesn't use AI plan reveal. Optional: AI-powered KYC document recognition (Onfido/Persona embed this internally — small CV models). Optional: AI fraud-detection during onboarding (Sift, ML-driven device fingerprinting). The Cal-AI-style theatrical AI reveal is OUT OF DOCTRINE in fintech because financial users perceive it as gimmicky and untrustworthy.
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment: 'Build a fintech onboarding FSM in XState v5 with 8-10 steps: WELCOME (1-2 screens, Sage register: "Investing made simple" NOT "Welcome to your money journey!"), AUTH (Sign in with Apple / Google / magic link — NO password), BIOMETRIC_ENROLL (Face ID / Touch ID setup immediately post-auth — item 61), PHONE_VERIFY (SMS OTP), BASIC_KYC (name, DOB, address, last-4 SSN), DOC_KYC (Onfido/Persona/Sumsub SDK iframe — ID upload + selfie liveness), EDUCATIONAL_INTERLEAVE ("While we verify, here''s how Round-Ups work" — converts wait-time to value), BANK_LINK (Plaid Link iframe), FUND_ACCOUNT (minimum-deposit prompt — the engineered aha), DASHBOARD (post-funding with first-feature CTA). Total 8-10 screens, 6-10 minutes. State persistence: backend (kyc_state, account_status); resume on cross-device. Biometric gate replaces password for repeat sessions. NO AI plan reveal. NO 33-step consumer questionnaire. Voice: Sage (financial wisdom) + Ruler (institutional authority). Aesthetic: solid backgrounds, no gradients, brand color OKLCH single accent (NOT indigo-500). Forbid: Liquid Glass translucency on KYC screens (signals insecurity), purple-blue gradients (consumer-app vibe in financial context), emoji bullets, 33-step questionnaires. Compliance: GDPR Article 9 + COPPA + GLBA + state-level money-transmitter consent disclosures rendered in plain language.' Second fragment: 'Build the BIOMETRIC_ENROLL screen — Face ID prompt with single Sage-register headline "Use Face ID to sign in securely" and a clear secondary skip with security trade-off explained. Use iOS LocalAuthentication framework or Android BiometricPrompt. Pair with passkey enrollment (WebAuthn/AuthenticationServices) for 2025-2026 default.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup an 8-step fintech onboarding. Step 1 WELCOME — single-screen value-prop "Invest your spare change" 32pt Söhne weight 600, ink #1A1816 on solid bone #F4EFE6. Single primary CTA "Get started" 14pt. NO illustration, NO gradient, NO 3D-render, NO emoji. Step 2 AUTH — Sign in with Apple top, Google second, "Use email" text-link below. Step 3 BIOMETRIC ENROLL — Face ID prompt with "Sign in securely with Face ID" headline and a small icon (NOT decorative — the iOS Face ID glyph). Step 4 PHONE — SMS OTP 6-digit input. Step 5 BASIC_KYC — name, DOB, address, last-4 SSN (US version) — single-column form, large inputs, NO multi-column density. Step 6 DOC_KYC — embed Onfido SDK or Persona Inquiry — full-screen camera capture for ID front, ID back, selfie liveness. Step 7 EDUCATIONAL_INTERLEAVE — "While we verify, here''s how Round-Ups work" with simple single-illustration explainer (NOT 3D blob, NOT gradient mesh — clean line illustration, ember accent). Step 8 BANK_LINK — Plaid Link iframe (Plaid handles UI). Step 9 FUND — "Add $5 to start investing" preset-amount chips (5, 10, 20, custom). Aesthetic: bank-grade trust — restrained typography, single ember-orange accent, solid backgrounds, NO Liquid Glass translucency on these screens (signals insecurity). Forbid: Inter-only font (use Söhne/Cabinet Grotesk), indigo-500/violet-500/purple-blue gradient (consumer-app vibe), emoji bullets, 3-column feature grids.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI typically (1) over-applies consumer-wellness doctrine to fintech (33-step questionnaire feels manipulative when SSN is involved), (2) skips the educational interleave during KYC (loses 25-pt completion lift), (3) defaults to password-based auth (2025-2026 default is biometric + magic link / SSO), (4) uses indigo-500 / purple-blue gradient on auth/KYC screens (signals untrustworthy consumer-app to financial users), (5) places paywall AT onboarding (out of doctrine; financial subscriptions are usage-gated), (6) uses Liquid Glass translucency on KYC screens (signals insecurity), (7) skips audit logging for KYC steps (compliance-blocking issue). Counter-prompt: 'Build a fintech onboarding with 8-10 named steps (welcome, auth, biometric_enroll, phone_verify, basic_kyc, doc_kyc, educational_interleave, bank_link, fund_account, dashboard). Use Sage/Ruler voice — confident, institutional. Replace password with magic link + biometric gate. Educational interleave between KYC steps — convert wait-time to value. NO AI plan reveal, NO 33-step questionnaire, NO paywall at onboarding. Solid backgrounds, no gradients, brand color OKLCH single accent. Audit-log every KYC step (compliance requirement). Use Onfido or Persona SDK for ID/selfie KYC; Plaid Link for bank linking.'
anti_patterns: (1) 33-step consumer questionnaire (manipulative-feeling when SSN involved). (2) AI plan reveal at climax (gimmicky in financial context). (3) Paywall at onboarding (out of doctrine — defer to feature-gating). (4) Password-based auth (insecure-feeling in 2025-2026 fintech). (5) Skipping educational interleave during KYC (loses completion uplift). (6) Hidden material terms (FTC Click-to-Cancel + state laws + GLBA disclosure requirements). (7) Liquid Glass translucent KYC screens (signals insecurity). (8) Aggressive sunk-cost extraction (Cal-AI doctrine — KYC steps are mandatory friction, not extraction tactic). (9) Generic Lucide icon trinity on auth screens (consumer-app vibe). (10) Caregiver/Jester voice in financial copy (undermines trust; use Sage/Ruler). (11) Skipping FINRA suitability questionnaire (Robinhood) for investing apps — regulatory violation. (12) Storing biometric data server-side (must stay device-local; iOS Secure Enclave / Android Trusted Execution).
regulatory_risk: High in all jurisdictions. (a) US: BSA/AML (Bank Secrecy Act); FinCEN registration for money transmitters; OFAC sanctions screening; KYC/CIP under Patriot Act; FINRA suitability for investing apps; SEC for securities; CFPB for consumer financial products; state money-transmitter licenses (50-state licensure for fintech is standard); GLBA financial privacy; FTC enforcement (recent: Cash App $175M settlement Jan 2025 for deceptive marketing). (b) EU: PSD2 strong customer authentication; AMLD5/AMLD6 KYC; GDPR Article 9 financial data; MiCA for crypto; DORA operational resilience; consumer credit directive. (c) UK: FCA authorization; PSR for payments; CMA for competition. (d) PCI DSS for card data. (e) SOC 2 Type II expected. (f) State-level (NY DFS BitLicense for crypto, CA DFPI). (g) Underage user protections — fintech apps cannot serve under-13 (COPPA); under-18 typically requires parent-supervised account (custodial Robinhood, Acorns Early). FTC enforcement against Cash App ($175M Jan 2025) and Robinhood SEC settlement ($65M, gamification of trading, 2020) are active precedents.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple Guideline 3.1.1 (IAP) — fintech apps generally exempt for transactional financial services (deposits, transfers, investing) but PREMIUM SUBSCRIPTION TIERS require IAP. Apple Guideline 3.1.5(a) — physical-services exemption for banking. Apple Guideline 5.1.1 — privacy policy + data minimization mandatory. Apple Guideline 4.8 — Sign in with Apple required if other social logins offered. Google Play Financial Services Policy — strict KYC/AML compliance verification. Play Data Safety form must declare financial data. Apple App Tracking Transparency — IDFA collection requires explicit prompt. EU PSD2 SCA mandates strong customer authentication for transactions >€30 — biometric gate satisfies. EU eIDAS 2.0 (2026) introduces digital identity wallets for cross-border KYC.
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 AA mandatory (banking falls under ADA in US; EU EAA 2025 enforcement). Screen-reader compatibility critical for KYC screens (visually-impaired users opening accounts). Camera-based KYC (selfie liveness) must offer alternative path (in-person verification, document mailing) — common ADA failure. Touch target 24×24 px (SC 2.5.8) — common failure on dense KYC forms. Color contrast 4.5:1 (SC 1.4.3) — financial UIs often use grey-on-grey for fine print, violating. Sufficient time (SC 2.2.1) — timed OTP flows must be extendable. Redundant entry (SC 3.3.7, new in 2.2) — KYC must not re-ask info already provided. Animation (SC 2.3.3) — biometric Face ID animation must respect prefers-reduced-motion. EU EAA 2025 banking enforcement applies.
pitfalls_caveats: (1) Money-transmitter licensing is the deepest moat; solo-devs typically partner with BaaS (Treasury Prime, Unit) — adds revenue share but enables compliance. (2) KYC false-positive rate must balance fraud prevention vs friction (Onfido optimal ~5-8% reject rate). (3) Plaid integration sometimes fails for small/regional banks — fallback to manual ACH routing/account input required. (4) Biometric data MUST stay device-local (iOS Secure Enclave / Android TEE) — server-side biometric storage = major regulatory violation. (5) Video selfie liveness has 5-10% device-camera failure rate on older Androids. (6) International expansion = per-country licensing (each EU state, UK FCA, Canada provincial); KYC vendor selection by jurisdiction. (7) Synapse bankruptcy April 2024 disrupted multiple BaaS-dependent fintechs — diligence on BaaS partner solvency. (8) Crypto-fintech compliance differs (state BitLicense, MiCA in EU); often easier to fail-fast if not prepared.
cross_cultural_validity: EU strict (PSD2 SCA, AMLD5/AMLD6, GDPR Article 9 financial data) — onboarding longer than US, video-selfie KYC standard. UK FCA-authorized fintechs (Monzo, Revolut, Starling) have similar pattern + UK-specific Open Banking. LATAM: high under-banked population; mobile-fintech leaders (Nubank Brazil $50B IPO 2021, Mercado Pago, RappiPay) — onboarding emphasizes financial-inclusion / no-credit-history paths. MENA: Islamic-finance compliance (Sharia-compliant fintech — no interest-bearing products); Hala, Wahed Invest. India: Aadhaar-linked KYC (digital ID) accelerates onboarding to ~3 minutes; UPI integration mandatory. CN: AntFinancial / Alipay / WeChat Pay closed market; foreign fintech essentially excluded. JP: regulatory caution (FSA Financial Services Agency); J-Coin Pay, PayPay. AU/NZ: AUSTRAC compliance; Up Bank pattern. Africa: M-Pesa pattern (mobile-money-first) different from Western fintech.
prescribed_action: Use Trust-First Sequenced Onboarding — minimal welcome (1-2 screens) → magic-link/SSO auth (item 60, NO password) → biometric gate enrollment (item 61) → phone OTP → basic KYC (name/DOB/address/SSN-last-4) → document KYC via Onfido/Persona/Sumsub SDK → EDUCATIONAL INTERLEAVE during KYC verification ('How Round-Ups work' style) — converts wait-time to value (Acorns +25pt completion lift) → bank link via Plaid/Tink → fund-account aha → dashboard with first-feature CTA. NO AI plan reveal at climax. NO 33-step consumer questionnaire. NO paywall at onboarding (defer to usage-feature gating). Voice: Sage + Ruler — confident, institutional, financial-wisdom — NOT Caregiver/Jester. Aesthetic: solid backgrounds, no gradients, single brand accent OKLCH (forbid indigo-500), bank-grade restrained typography. Audit log every KYC step for FinCEN/SAR compliance. State persistence backend-sourced for cross-device resume. Biometric data device-local only.
questionnaire_relevance: FINRA suitability questionnaire (investing apps only, regulatory) — 5-7 questions about investment experience, risk tolerance, goals. NOT a sunk-cost extraction; framed as financial-planning. Forbid demographic-heavy openings beyond KYC requirements. Conversational form (item 28) for suitability questions; form-style (item 28) for KYC fields (DMV-style acceptable in financial-form context).
aha_relevance: Aha = funded account (Acorns) or first-transaction (Cash App, Venmo, Robinhood). Time-to-aha 5-10 minutes including KYC. The educational interleave during KYC creates a SECONDARY mini-aha (understanding the product mechanic) that reduces KYC abandonment. NO theatrical AI reveal — the funded-account moment IS the aha.
telemetry_events: onboarding_step_viewed, onboarding_step_completed, auth_method_used, biometric_enrolled, phone_verified, basic_kyc_submitted, doc_kyc_started, doc_kyc_completed, doc_kyc_rejected, educational_interleave_shown, bank_link_started, bank_link_completed, account_funded, first_transaction_completed. Compliance audit events: kyc_initiated, kyc_provider_response_received, sanctions_screening_passed, ofac_screening_passed, sar_filing_triggered. Retain 5-7 years per BSA.
measurement_horizon: Day 0 (KYC completion + funded account = activation); Day 30 (D30 retention for funded vs non-funded — primary leading indicator); 12-month LTV ($200-2000 range depending on product).
emotional_archetype_alignment: PRIMARY: Sage (financial wisdom, institutional knowledge — Acorns, Wealthfront, Betterment). SECONDARY: Ruler (institutional authority, security — Chase, Goldman Sachs Marcus). TERTIARY: Caregiver (financial care for novices — Acorns Early, Greenlight kids). MISMATCHES to avoid: Outlaw (rebellion clashes with regulatory compliance — though Robinhood positioned as financial-democratization-Outlaw and got SEC fined $65M in 2020 for it), Jester (humor undermines financial trust), Magician (theatrical AI reveal feels gimmicky and untrustworthy in financial context).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Long Questionnaire / Sunk-Cost Pattern (item 17) — KYC is mandatory friction, NOT sunk-cost extraction. Pairs against Story-Onboarding Doctrine (item 18) — Sage register over Hero arc. Pairs against Premature Authentication (auth happens early but is justified by financial trust prerequisite, NOT pre-value-demo). Pairs against Wasted Loading (educational interleave converts KYC wait-time into value), Contextless Permissions (camera permission for ID/selfie KYC needs priming).
